mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive quarterly returns with Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods show no significant negative correlation with 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 2.50 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 2.50 are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightness and wage inflation pressures, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant predictive power for quarterly equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signals weakening residential construction demand and reduced economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 1.0 million are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances.
